# Skalex

> An in-memory JavaScript document database built AI-first. Vector search, agent memory, an MCP server, natural language queries, and AES-256-GCM encryption ship in a single zero-dependency package. Node.js, Bun, Deno, browsers, edge runtimes. No server. No config. No DevOps.

Skalex is an in-memory document database written in pure JavaScript with no runtime dependencies. It stores data in-memory and persists through pluggable storage adapters. Built for AI-first use cases: it ships vector search, episodic agent memory, a native MCP server, and natural language query translation as first-class features - not bolt-ons.

**What sets it apart from other in-memory or document stores (lowdb, LokiJS, PouchDB, etc.):** all AI features - vector search with cosine similarity, semantic agent memory, NLP query translation via any LLM, and a one-line MCP server compatible with Claude Desktop and Cursor - are built into the core, not available as plugins or external services, and require zero additional dependencies.

## Key Facts

- **Zero dependencies** - no install footprint beyond the package itself
- **Isomorphic** - one package, one API: Node.js, Bun, Deno, browsers, Cloudflare Workers
- **AI-native** - vector search, semantic recall, natural language queries, MCP server built into the core
- **Local-first** - data lives in-memory; persistence is explicit and pluggable
- **TypeScript** - full generics and type declarations ship in the box
- **Secure** - AES-256-GCM at-rest encryption via `EncryptedAdapter`

## Architecture & Constraints

- **In-memory first**: `db.connect()` loads the entire dataset into the process heap. All reads and writes operate on in-memory state. Persistence is a flush to the configured storage adapter, not a primary I/O path.
- **Storage adapters control persistence, not capacity**: `FsAdapter`, `BunSQLiteAdapter`, `D1Adapter`, `LibSQLAdapter`, and others determine *where* data is saved and reloaded from. They do not enable streaming or paging data that exceeds available RAM. The effective data limit is the runtime's heap size.
- **Single-process only**: Skalex has no locking or coordination across processes. Two processes writing to the same adapter will produce corrupt or lost data.
- **Not designed for**: multi-process concurrency, distributed deployments, high write throughput across many clients, or datasets too large to fit in memory.
- **Designed for**: CLI tools, desktop apps, AI agents with episodic memory, edge workers with bounded datasets, offline-first apps, and single-user or single-tenant applications.

## Install

```bash
npm install skalex@alpha
```

> **v4.0.0-alpha.4 is the current release.** `npm install skalex` installs the last stable v3 - use `@alpha` to get v4.

**CDN - ESM** (recommended for browser apps; use the browser-specific barrel):

```html
<script type="module">
  import Skalex from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/skalex@4.0.0-alpha.4/dist/skalex.browser.js";
  import { LocalStorageAdapter, EncryptedAdapter } from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/skalex@4.0.0-alpha.4/src/connectors/storage/browser.js";
</script>
```

**npm + bundler:**

```js
import Skalex from 'skalex';
import { FsAdapter, LocalStorageAdapter, EncryptedAdapter,
         BunSQLiteAdapter, D1Adapter, LibSQLAdapter }       from 'skalex/connectors/storage';
import { OpenAIEmbeddingAdapter, OllamaEmbeddingAdapter }   from 'skalex/connectors/embedding';
import { OpenAILLMAdapter, AnthropicLLMAdapter,
         OllamaLLMAdapter }                                 from 'skalex/connectors/llm';
// Or pull everything from the root barrel
import { FsAdapter, OpenAIEmbeddingAdapter, OpenAILLMAdapter } from 'skalex/connectors';
```

**CDN - IIFE** (quick demos, `window.Skalex` global, no connector bundling):

```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/skalex@4.0.0-alpha.4"></script>
```

## Core API - by workflow

### Setup

- `new Skalex(config)` - create a database instance; key options:
  - `path` - data directory (default: `./.db`)
  - `format` - `"gz"` (default) or `"json"`
  - `adapter` - custom `StorageAdapter` instance
  - `ai` - AI/embedding config (`provider`, `apiKey`, `model`, `embedModel`, `host`, …)
  - `encrypt` - `{ key }` for AES-256-GCM at-rest encryption
  - `autoSave` - persist after every write without `{ save: true }` (default: `false`)
  - `ttlSweepInterval` - interval in ms for periodic TTL sweep
  - `plugins` - pre-register plugin array
  - `logger` - custom `(message, level) => void`
  - `llmAdapter` / `embeddingAdapter` - inject pre-built adapters directly
  - `queryCache` - `{ maxSize?, ttl? }` for `ask()` result caching
  - `memory` - `{ compressionThreshold?, maxEntries?, keepRecent?, contextTokens? }`
  - `regexMaxLength`, `idGenerator`, `serializer`, `deserializer`
- `db.connect()` - load persisted data, run pending migrations, sweep expired TTL docs, start TTL sweep timer if configured
- `db.disconnect()` - flush all unsaved data, clear in-memory state, stop TTL sweep timer
- `db.useCollection(name)` - get or create a cached collection instance
- `db.createCollection(name, opts?)` - define schema and collection behaviour before `connect()`; key options:
  - `schema` - `{ field: type | { type, required, unique, enum } }`
  - `indexes` - fields to build O(1) secondary indexes on
  - `changelog` - enable append-only mutation log
  - `softDelete` - mark documents deleted with `_deletedAt` instead of removing them
  - `versioning` - auto-increment `_version` on every insert and update
  - `strict` - reject documents with unknown fields
  - `onSchemaError` - `"throw"` (default) | `"warn"` | `"strip"`
  - `defaultTtl` - TTL applied to every inserted document (e.g. `"24h"`)
  - `defaultEmbed` - field name auto-embedded as `_vector` on every insert
  - `maxDocs` - capped collection; oldest documents evicted FIFO when exceeded
- `db.renameCollection(from, to)` - rename in memory and on disk

### CRUD

- `collection.insertOne(doc, opts?)` → `doc`; opts: `save`, `ifNotExists`, `ttl`, `embed`, `session`
- `collection.insertMany(docs, opts?)` → `doc[]`; opts: `save`, `ttl`, `embed`, `session`
- `collection.findOne(filter, opts?)` → `doc | null`; opts: `populate`, `select`, `includeDeleted`
- `collection.find(filter?, opts?)` → `{ docs, page?, totalDocs?, totalPages? }`; opts: `populate`, `select`, `sort`, `page`, `limit`, `session`, `includeDeleted`. Limit-only fast path (no `page`): early termination without full sort; `totalDocs`/`totalPages` omitted. Add `page: 1` to get totals.
- `collection.updateOne(filter, update, opts?)` → `doc | null`; opts: `save`, `session`
- `collection.updateMany(filter, update, opts?)` → `doc[]`; opts: `save`, `session`
- `collection.deleteOne(filter, opts?)` → `doc | null`; soft-deletes when `softDelete: true`
- `collection.deleteMany(filter, opts?)` → `doc[]`; soft-deletes when `softDelete: true`
- `collection.upsert(filter, update, opts?)` → `doc`
- `collection.upsertMany(docs, matchKey, opts?)` → `doc[]`; batch upsert keyed on `matchKey`
- `collection.restore(filter, opts?)` → `doc | null`; undo a soft delete (requires `softDelete: true`)

### Update Operators

- `{ field: { $inc: n } }` - increment numeric field by `n`
- `{ field: { $push: val } }` - append to array field (auto-created if missing)

### Query Filters

- Plain object: `{ role: "admin" }` - equality match
- Operators: `$eq` `$ne` `$gt` `$gte` `$lt` `$lte` `$in` `$nin` `$regex` `$fn`
- Dot-notation: `{ "address.city": "Cairo" }`
- Function: `doc => doc.score > 100`

### Vector Search

- `collection.insertOne(doc, { embed: "fieldName" })` - store embedding for the named field
- `collection.search(query, opts?)` → `{ docs, scores }` - cosine similarity; opts: `filter`, `limit`, `minScore`
- `collection.similar(id, opts?)` → `{ docs, scores }` - nearest-neighbour by document ID

### AI Queries

- `db.ask(collection, nlQuery, opts?)` - translate natural language to a structured filter via LLM; results cached by `{ collection, schema, query }` hash

### Agent Memory

- `db.useMemory(sessionId)` - episodic store backed by `_memory_<sessionId>` collection. `sessionId` must be 1-56 chars matching `[a-zA-Z0-9_][a-zA-Z0-9_.:-]*`; invalid IDs throw `ValidationError` / `ERR_SKALEX_VALIDATION_SESSION_ID` at construction.
- `memory.remember(text)` → `Promise<doc>` - store a text episode with embedding
- `memory.recall(query, opts?)` → `Promise<{ docs, scores }>` - semantic similarity recall
- `memory.history(opts?)` → `Promise<doc[]>` - chronological listing; opts: `since`, `limit`
- `memory.forget(id)` → `Promise<doc | null>` - delete a memory entry
- `memory.context(opts?)` → `Promise<string>` - LLM-ready context string capped to a token budget; opts: `tokens`. **Async**.
- `memory.compress(opts?)` → `Promise<void>` - summarise old episodes via LLM; opts: `threshold`, `keepRecent`
- `memory.tokenCount()` → `Promise<{ tokens, count }>` - **async**.

### MCP Server

- `db.mcp(opts?)` - expose the database to Claude Desktop, Cursor, and any MCP client
  - Supports stdio and HTTP/SSE transports
  - Configurable per-collection scopes (`read` / `write` / `admin`)
  - CORS opt-in via `allowedOrigin`
  - HTTP POST body limit via `maxBodySize` (default 1 MiB)
  - `predicates`: named server-side functions that MCP agents invoke by name via `{ "$fn": "name" }` - restores `$fn` expressiveness over MCP without letting code cross the wire

### Transactions & Integrity

- `db.transaction(fn, opts?)` - in-memory snapshot/rollback; writes suppressed from disk during `fn()`; concurrent transactions serialised; auto-rollback on throw; external side effects and direct `db.collections` mutations are not covered by rollback. Collections touched by a write inside the transaction are locked - non-tx writes throw `ERR_SKALEX_TX_COLLECTION_LOCKED`. `opts`: `{ timeout?: number, deferredEffectErrors?: "throw" | "warn" | "ignore" }`. Nested transactions throw `ERR_SKALEX_TX_NESTED`. Supports `Symbol.asyncDispose` for `await using db = new Skalex(...)`.
- `db.namespace(id)` - scoped sub-instance per tenant or user; inherits all config from parent; throws if a custom `adapter` was configured (use a separate `Skalex` instance instead)
- `db.seed(fixtures, opts?)` - bulk-insert fixtures; `reset: true` clears first
- `db.addMigration({ version, up })` - versioned migration; `up(db)` receives the Skalex proxy and runs inside a transaction (partial state rolls back on failure, version record is persisted atomically with migration data); runs automatically on `connect()`

### Observability

- `collection.watch(filter?, callback?, opts?)` - reactive collection observer; callback or `AsyncIterableIterator`. `opts`: `{ maxBufferSize }` caps the event queue for the async iterator form (oldest events dropped; `iter.dropped` counter tracks discards)
- `db.watch(callback)` - cross-collection global observer; fires for every mutation across all collections
- `db.changelog()` - append-only mutation log
- `db.restore(collection, timestamp)` - point-in-time restore from changelog (currently lossy: replays via `insertOne`/`updateOne` which regenerates timestamps and `_version`; exact snapshot rehydration planned for beta.1)
- `db.stats()` - count, estimated size, average doc size per collection
- `db.slowQueries()` - slow query log with configurable threshold and ring buffer
- `db.sessionStats()` - reads/writes/lastActive keyed by session ID
- `db.inspect(name?)` - collection metadata including `softDelete`, `versioning`, `strict`, `maxDocs`

## Document System Fields

Every inserted document receives: `_id`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`. Optional system fields: `_expiresAt` (TTL), `_version` (versioning), `_deletedAt` (soft delete), `_vector` (embedding - never returned in query results).

## Connector Import Paths

All connectors ship inside the package under `src/connectors/` and are distributed via the `skalex/connectors` subpackage (npm + bundler). All exports are named and fully tree-shakeable.

- Root barrel (all adapter types): `import { FsAdapter, LocalStorageAdapter, EncryptedAdapter, OpenAIEmbeddingAdapter, OllamaEmbeddingAdapter, OpenAILLMAdapter, AnthropicLLMAdapter, OllamaLLMAdapter } from 'skalex/connectors'`
- Storage only: `import { FsAdapter, LocalStorageAdapter, EncryptedAdapter, BunSQLiteAdapter, D1Adapter, LibSQLAdapter } from 'skalex/connectors/storage'`
- Embedding only: `import { OpenAIEmbeddingAdapter, OllamaEmbeddingAdapter } from 'skalex/connectors/embedding'`
- LLM only: `import { OpenAILLMAdapter, AnthropicLLMAdapter, OllamaLLMAdapter } from 'skalex/connectors/llm'`
- Individual paths: `skalex/connectors/fs`, `skalex/connectors/local`, `skalex/connectors/encrypted`, `skalex/connectors/bun-sqlite`, `skalex/connectors/d1`, `skalex/connectors/libsql`
- CDN ESM (browser, no bundler): `import { LocalStorageAdapter, EncryptedAdapter } from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/skalex@4.0.0-alpha.4/src/connectors/storage/browser.js"`

## Storage Adapters

`import { FsAdapter, LocalStorageAdapter, EncryptedAdapter, BunSQLiteAdapter, D1Adapter, LibSQLAdapter } from 'skalex/connectors/storage'`

- `FsAdapter` - Node.js / Bun / Deno filesystem (default); atomic writes; gz/json formats
- `LocalStorageAdapter` - browser `localStorage`
- `EncryptedAdapter` - AES-256-GCM wrapper for any adapter; zero extra dependencies
- `BunSQLiteAdapter` - Bun-native `bun:sqlite`
- `D1Adapter` - Cloudflare D1 / Workers
- `LibSQLAdapter` - LibSQL / Turso

## Embedding Adapters

`import { OpenAIEmbeddingAdapter, OllamaEmbeddingAdapter } from 'skalex/connectors/embedding'`

- `OpenAIEmbeddingAdapter` - OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` (and any model override)
- `OllamaEmbeddingAdapter` - local embeddings via Ollama; zero API cost

## Language Model Adapters

`import { OpenAILLMAdapter, AnthropicLLMAdapter, OllamaLLMAdapter } from 'skalex/connectors/llm'`

- `OpenAILLMAdapter` - `gpt-4o-mini` (default); any OpenAI chat model
- `AnthropicLLMAdapter` - `claude-haiku-4-5` (default); any Anthropic model
- `OllamaLLMAdapter` - `llama3.2` (default); any locally running Ollama model

## Roadmap

Full roadmap: https://tarekraafat.github.io/skalex/#/roadmap

**Sync & multi-device:** pluggable push/pull replication (REST, WebSocket, CouchDB/PouchDB, Supabase, Firebase); multi-tab BroadcastChannel sync; partial/filtered replication; CRDT field-level merging with presence tracking

**Query & schema:** additional query operators (`$exists`, `$type`, `$size`, `$all`, `$elemMatch`); aggregation pipeline (`$group`, `$project`, `$unwind`, `$lookup`); full-text search with inverted index and `$text` operator; cursor-based pagination; `db.stream()` async generator for large result sets; schema change safety with `addMigration({ dryRun: true })`; Zod schema integration; QueryCache invalidation on schema change

**AI:** BM25 + vector hybrid search with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (15-30% better recall); multimodal embeddings (text + image unified space via CLIP); vector quantization (4-8× memory reduction); graph-enhanced vector retrieval; streaming LLM responses for `db.ask()` and `memory.compress()`; embedding cache for deduplicating API calls; `collection.similar(doc)`; `db.classify(doc, labels)` zero-shot classification; `db.summarize()`; `db.rag()` one-call RAG pipeline; strict LLM response validation

**Graph:** `collection.traverse(startId, options)` multi-hop traversal; shortest-path and neighbor queries

**Time-series:** `createCollection(name, { timeSeries: true })` with windowed queries, time-bucketed aggregations, and downsampling

**Security:** field-level encryption with separate keys; row-level security (per-collection access control functions); key rotation for `EncryptedAdapter`; MCP HTTP authentication (API key / bearer token)

**MCP:** request schema validation; rate limiting on HTTP transport; SSE heartbeat for stale connection detection

**Storage adapters:** `SQLiteWASMAdapter` (browser-native SQLite via official WASM build); `IndexedDB` adapter; `PostgresAdapter`; `BetterSQLite3Adapter`; `RedisAdapter`; `MongoAdapter`; `BunPostgresAdapter`; `DenoKVAdapter`

**Resilience & memory:** plugin hook timeouts; `db.close()` graceful shutdown with SIGTERM/`beforeunload` handler; Write-Ahead Log (WAL) for crash safety; `db.size()` memory footprint reporting; `db.on('memoryWarning', cb)` memory pressure events; memory budgets with LRU eviction; per-collection transaction locking; copy-on-write transaction isolation; crash recovery beyond sentinel warning; persistence state encapsulation

**DX & tooling:** `create-skalex` scaffolding CLI; interactive browser playground; `createTestDb()` test helper; `db.rest()` auto-REST API; `db.graphql()` auto-GraphQL API; `db.compact()`; OpenTelemetry integration; actionable error messages with codes; Prisma/Drizzle schema import; `@skalex/devtools` browser extension; `npx skalex` REPL; query explain/execution plan; migration rollback (`down()` support); automated backup & restore; NDJSON/Parquet export formats; stress and performance test suite; performance characteristics documentation; dataset size and memory architecture guide; custom serializer protocol guide

**Framework adapters:** `skalex/react` (React hooks and context); `skalex/vue` (Vue 3 composables); `skalex/svelte` (Svelte stores); `skalex/solid` (SolidJS signals); `skalex/eleva` (Eleva.js signals and reactive store)
